evals.prompt: Analyze a PDF paper containing model architecture, loss functions, and detection flow to generate a complete patent draft. Output separate files for claims, specification, abstract, and abstract drawing. All core formulas must be native editable Word formulas, and every claim feature must be traceable back to paper page numbers or formulas.
evals.expected_output: A complete patent package with stable source IDs, evidence ledger, claim-feature map, editable equations, a claim-aligned main flowchart, methodology figures, separate DOCX files, and passing validation reports.

evals.prompt: The paper is a scanned PDF, and some formulas are unclear from OCR. Please first organize the confirmable technical solutions and generate a draft of claims; do not guess uncertain formula symbols.
evals.expected_output: An OCR-aware claim draft that marks uncertain source blocks, excludes unsupported limitations from formal claims, lists specific inventor questions, and does not fabricate equation symbols.

evals.prompt: Comparing the paper with an existing patent, evaluate item by item whether the patent claims are supported in the paper, and annotate content that is paper-only, patent-only, or where the patent is broader or narrower.
evals.expected_output: Separate source maps plus a bidirectional feature table using the declared relationship labels, exact locators for both documents, and no assumption that patent-only text is paper-supported.
